title: Data Center POWER eXchange
content:
Data Center POWER eXchange, a conference focused on power generation challenges in digital infrastructure, will be held September 30 to October 1, 2026, at the Omni Shoreham in Washington, D.C.

The event brings together utilities, data center operators, developers, policymakers, and investors to address electricity demand driven by artificial intelligence and rapid data center expansion. The conference is organized by POWER magazine, which has covered the power generation industry for more than a century.

The program centers on three areas: grid interconnection and transmission capacity; on-site power generation and energy mix strategies; and scaling infrastructure to meet accelerating demand. The conference takes place in Washington, D.C., one of the fastest-growing data center regions in the United States, positioning participants at the intersection of energy policy and infrastructure planning.

The 2026 edition follows the 2025 conference, which organizers say generated discussions on power infrastructure and data center expansion trends. The event is part of POWER magazine's portfolio of conferences and publications, which also includes Chemical Engineering, Experience POWER, CLEAN Americas, and POWER.

Registration for the 2026 event is open, with options available for attendees to exhibit, sponsor, or book accommodations at the hotel venue.

Source: https://www.datacenterpowerexchange.com/

title: Saudi Arabia Innovates to Support Renewable Energy Goals
content:
Saudi Arabia is pursuing advanced cooling technologies for high-efficiency solar cells to support its renewable energy targets, according to research aligned with the country's Vision 2030 strategy.

Triple-junction photovoltaic cells, which offer superior energy conversion capabilities, face operational challenges in high-temperature environments including thermal degradation and reduced performance. Effective thermal management is critical to sustaining efficiency and reliability in these systems, particularly in Saudi Arabia's arid climate where high ambient temperatures significantly reduce output.

Saudi Arabia has set a target to generate 50% of its electricity from renewable sources by 2030, requiring development of 130 GW of renewable energy capacity. This includes approximately 58.7 GW from solar energy and 40 GW from wind energy.

Active cooling systems integrated into photovoltaic installations can enhance solar generation efficiency and reliability by mitigating temperature-related performance losses. Researchers working on thermal management solutions say the technology aligns with Vision 2030's objectives of economic diversification and fostering a knowledge-based economy.

The country's vast solar irradiance potential makes the region well-suited for advanced PV deployment, provided durability and efficiency challenges are addressed. By developing and adopting efficient cooling solutions for solar installations, Saudi Arabia aims to position itself as a leader in renewable energy technologies while reducing dependence on oil revenues.

The renewable energy expansion forms a key pillar of Vision 2030, which prioritizes sustainability and economic diversification through public service sector development.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/saudi-arabia-innovates-to-support-renewable-energy-goals/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/saudi-arabia-innovates-to-support-renewable-energy-goals/ "Saudi Arabia Innovates to Support Renewable Energy Goals"

title: Carbon Capture Projects Come Online in Iceland, Saudi Arabia
content:
Carbon capture facilities came online in Iceland and Saudi Arabia as companies advance technologies to reduce industrial emissions and remove carbon dioxide from the atmosphere.

Iceland inaugurated Steingerdur, a carbon capture installation at the ON Power geothermal plant, which the government said will deliver about 10% of Iceland's 2030 Climate Action Plan targets for the energy and industrial sectors. The facility uses direct water capture to remove carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide emissions from the geothermal steam cycle, then stores the captured minerals underground where they turn to rock in approximately two years using technology from Carbfix, a carbon mineralization company.

Iceland is also home to Orca, a direct air capture and storage plant operated by Switzerland-based Climeworks that began operating in September 2021.

In Saudi Arabia, Climeworks, the King Abdullah Petroleum Studies and Research Center (KAPSARC), and the Saudi Energy Ministry inaugurated a direct air capture testing unit at KAPSARC's facilities in Riyadh. The mobile unit captures carbon dioxide directly from the atmosphere and is validating Climeworks' technology under the region's hot and arid conditions.

Climeworks opened a U.S. headquarters in Austin, Texas last year. ON Power operates geothermal and hydropower plants in Iceland and is part of Reykjavik Energy, which also owns Carbfix.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/carbon-capture-projects-come-online-in-iceland-saudi-arabia/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/carbon-capture-projects-come-online-in-iceland-saudi-arabia/ "Carbon Capture Projects Come Online in Iceland, Saudi Arabia"

title: Aramco, Rondo Energy Studying GW-Scale Thermal Storage, Hydrogen, Carbon Capture Deployment
content:
Saudi Aramco and Rondo Energy have launched engineering studies for an industrial-scale thermal energy storage project that could eventually expand to 1 GWh capacity across Aramco's global facilities.

The companies signed a memorandum of understanding on May 16 to deploy Rondo's heat battery technology at Aramco operations, aimed at reducing operating costs and supporting the Saudi oil and chemicals company's carbon emissions reduction plans. The project will assess scaling to 1 GWh with potential for additional deployment at multiple Aramco sites worldwide.

The partners plan to explore joint development of novel applications using Rondo's heat battery technology, including hydrogen production and carbon capture capabilities. Rondo's heat batteries store energy from wind and solar power and deliver it at the high temperatures and gigawatt scale needed for industrial processes.

Ali Al-Meshari, Aramco senior vice president of Technology, Oversight and Coordination, said the company has set a goal to achieve net-zero Scope 1 and Scope 2 operational greenhouse gas emissions by 2050 across its wholly owned and operated assets. "Innovative technologies deployed at scale are needed to reduce the costs of emissions reduction," Al-Meshari said.

Aramco Ventures made an equity investment in Rondo Energy in July 2023. The California-based company provides lower-carbon industrial heat and power solutions and specializes in thermal energy storage technology.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/aramco-rondo-energy-studying-gw-scale-thermal-storage-hydrogen-carbon-capture-deployment/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/aramco-rondo-energy-studying-gw-scale-thermal-storage-hydrogen-carbon-capture-deployment/ "Aramco, Rondo Energy Studying GW-Scale Thermal Storage, Hydrogen, Carbon Capture Deployment"

title: Saudi Group Set to Build 10-GW Wind Farm in Egypt
content:
A Saudi-based energy company signed an agreement with Egyptian authorities on July 17 to develop a 10-GW wind farm west of Sohag, marking the next phase of a renewable energy project first outlined in 2022.

ACWA Power and Egypt's New and Renewable Energy Authority signed a memorandum of understanding allocating approximately 3,000 square kilometers of land for the installation. The project follows an initial agreement signed by ACWA Power, the Egyptian Electricity Transmission Co., and the NREA in November 2022.

Egypt's Minister of Electricity and Renewable Energy Mohamed Shaker Al-Marqabi said the country aims to increase renewable energy capacity to 42% of its energy mix by 2035. "Egypt has adopted an ambitious program to advance the electricity sector in various fields, which includes maximizing the utilization of new and renewable energy resources," Al-Marqabi said.

The wind farm is expected to save Egypt approximately $6.5 billion annually compared to natural gas-based power generation, according to officials. The project aligns with Egypt's Vision 2030 and National Climate Strategy 2050, both aimed at reducing carbon emissions and supporting sustainable economic growth.

Al-Marqabi characterized the development as an extension of relations between Egypt and Saudi Arabia. He noted that Egypt's availability of land and comparative advantages position the country to produce renewable energy for domestic consumption and potential export.

Egypt has been reducing its reliance on natural gas for electricity generation as part of broader renewable energy expansion efforts.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/saudi-group-set-to-build-10-gw-wind-farm-in-egypt/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/saudi-group-set-to-build-10-gw-wind-farm-in-egypt/ "Saudi Group Set to Build 10-GW Wind Farm in Egypt"

title: China, Saudi Arabia Team on Wind Projects in Uzbekistan
content:
China's Envision Energy will supply 1 GW of generation capacity for wind projects in Uzbekistan designed by Saudi Arabia's ACWA Power, the Shanghai-based turbine manufacturer announced June 20.

Envision, China's second-largest wind turbine manufacturer, will provide its EN171-6.5 turbines to ACWA's Bash and Dzhankeldy onshore wind farms. The announcement follows several major turbine deals by Envision in recent months across Egypt, India and Saudi Arabia.

Earlier this month, Envision secured an order to supply approximately 1.7 GW of turbines to Saudi Arabia's Neom hydrogen facility. That $8.4 billion project will link 4 GW of solar, wind and battery energy storage to produce up to 600 tonnes daily of green ammonia using more than 2 GW of hydrogen electrolysis capacity.

BloombergNEF ranked Envision as the world's fourth-largest turbine supplier last year with 8.3 GW of installed generation capacity.

Uzbekistan is expanding renewable energy deployment to meet a target of installing at least 5 GW of onshore wind by 2030. ACWA Power signed a $10 billion investment cooperation agreement with Uzbekistan's Ministry of Energy in May to develop renewable energy, green hydrogen and gas-to-power projects across the country over the next five years.

The company also stands among China's leaders in energy management software development.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/china-saudi-arabia-team-on-wind-projects-in-uzbekistan/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/china-saudi-arabia-team-on-wind-projects-in-uzbekistan/ "China, Saudi Arabia Team on Wind Projects in Uzbekistan"

title: ACWA Power Inaugurates Renewable Energy Project
content:
ACWA Power inaugurated the 300 MW Sakaka photovoltaic project in Al Jouf, Saudi Arabia's first utility-scale renewable energy installation, with an investment value of 1.2 billion Saudi riyals.

The ceremony took place on April 9, 2021, under the patronage of Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud. Saudi Minister of Energy Prince Abdulaziz Bin Salman Bin Abdulaziz Al Saud and Al Jouf Governor Prince Faisal bin Nawaf bin Abdulaziz attended the event.

The Public Investment Fund holds 50% ownership of ACWA Power, the developer and operator. According to the Minister of Energy, 97% of the project's operating team are Saudi nationals, while 90% of the workforce comes from the Al Jouf region.

The Sakaka project was awarded to the ACWA Power-led consortium in 2018 at a record tariff of 8.781 halalas per kilowatt-hour at the time. The achievement helped establish pricing benchmarks for subsequent projects under Saudi Arabia's National Renewable Energy Program, reducing the cost of solar electricity generation in the Kingdom.

The project marks a significant step in Saudi Arabia's energy diversification strategy, which aims to reduce dependence on oil for domestic electricity generation and free more crude for export. The inauguration signals progress in the Kingdom's renewable energy ambitions as part of its broader Vision 2030 economic transformation plan.

ACWA Power operates power generation and water desalination facilities across thirteen countries.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-inaugurates-renewable-energy-project/

title: Doosan Škoda Power successfully launched two 200 MW turbines at the Fadhili power plant in Saudia Arabia
content:
Doosan Škoda Power has started operating two 200 MW turbines at the Fadhili steam-gas combined cycle power plant near Jubail in Saudi Arabia since March 2020. The turbines, DST-S10 condensation units with axial output and no reheating capability, account for 418 MW of the facility's total 1,509 MW capacity.

The Fadhili plant is jointly owned by Saudi Aramco, Saudi Electricity Company and French utility Engie. Of the plant's total output, 1,100 MW supplies electricity to local industry and residents, while the remaining 400 MW supports the plant's operations and gas extraction activities.

Doosan Škoda Power supplied the turbines along with associated equipment including pipelines, a control system with field equipment, steam compression and drainage systems, oil systems and high-pressure hydraulic systems. The project involved coordination with EPC supplier Doosan Heavy Industries & Construction Co.

Jindřich Závodný, the project manager, said the execution was completed smoothly and noted the installation addressed specific requirements including operation at 60 Hz frequency and compliance with local standards and seismic codes. "The installation and commissioning were supervised by our experienced technical advisers, who, together with their Korean colleagues, handed over the units for commercial operation," Závodný said in a statement.

The facility currently operates under a warranty period during which Doosan Škoda Power will provide customer support. This marks the company's first installation in Saudi Arabia.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/doosan-skoda-power-successfully-launched-two-200-mw-turbines-at-the-fadhili-power-plant-in-saudia-arabia/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/doosan-skoda-power-successfully-launched-two-200-mw-turbines-at-the-fadhili-power-plant-in-saudia-arabia/ "Doosan Škoda Power successfully launched two 200 MW turbines at the Fadhili power plant in Saudia Arabia"

title: Wärtsilä engine technology will enable Saudi Arabia mining site power plant to efficiently utilize solar energy
content:
Wärtsilä will supply engineering, equipment and technical advisory for a 44 MW power plant at the Mansourah & Massarah Gold Project mine in Saudi Arabia, marking the country's first newbuild power project to combine engine technology with solar energy in a hybrid concept.

Larsen & Toubro Ltd, the India-based main contractor, placed the order with Wärtsilä in March. The power plant will comprise six Wärtsilä 32 engines, with five operating and one on constant standby.

The Wärtsilä engine technology's flexibility allows full power output within minutes of startup, enabling the plant to efficiently utilize renewable solar energy. The rapid response capability addresses the inherent fluctuation of renewable energy sources such as solar and wind, allowing the plant to effectively balance electrical loads.

Amit Swarnkar, MENA Business Unit Head for the Metallurgical and Material Handling Division at Larsen & Toubro, said the company selected Wärtsilä technology for its capability to integrate photovoltaic solar into the system. "Reliability is key for any gold mine for securing uninterrupted operations and targeted revenues in all occasions," Swarnkar stated.

Alexandre Eykerman, Energy Business Director for the Middle East at Wärtsilä, said the project aligns with the company's approach to enabling sustainable energy supplies. "Maximising the use of renewable energy is central to Wärtsilä's approach to enabling sustainable energy supplies. We have extensive experience in providing power for mining operations, so this project fits our capabilities perfectly," Eykerman said.

Wärtsilä will deliver equipment on a fast-track basis within 10 months from contract signing. The plant is scheduled for commissioning by May 2021, with commercial operations beginning a year later.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-engine-technology-will-enable-saudi-arabia-mining-site-power-plant-to-efficiently-utilize-solar-energy/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-engine-technology-will-enable-saudi-arabia-mining-site-power-plant-to-efficiently-utilize-solar-energy/ "Wärtsilä engine technology will enable Saudi Arabia mining site power plant to efficiently utilize solar energy"

title: ACWA Power expands its geographic footprint by entering the Azerbaijan market
content:
ACWA Power signed an implementation agreement with Azerbaijan's Ministry of Energy to develop, build and operate a 240 MW wind power project, the Saudi Arabian energy company said on Thursday.

The project will operate under a 20-year power purchase agreement on a Build Own Operate basis, with the signing ceremony attended by Azerbaijan's Prime Minister Ali Asadov and Energy Minister Parviz Shahbazov.

The wind project represents one of the first two independent power projects in Azerbaijan and aligns with the government's strategy to diversify its energy mix away from oil and gas dependence. Azerbaijan has extensive renewable energy resources that remain largely untapped.

"We are pleased to sign this implementation agreement with the Government of Azerbaijan, which expands our geographic footprint of operations into this culturally rich and economically vibrant country straddling Europe and Asia," ACWA Power Chief Executive Officer Paddy Padmanathan said in a statement.

Padmanathan added that the renewable energy investment reinforces ACWA Power's role in deploying Saudi foreign investment to support decarbonisation of electricity generation sectors globally.

ACWA Power operates power generation and water desalination plants across the Middle East, North Africa and Asia. The company will leverage its expertise in renewable energy project management to reduce generation costs and provide affordable energy access to Azerbaijani communities, according to the statement.

The agreement marks ACWA Power's entry into the Azerbaijani energy market and underscores deepening economic ties between Saudi Arabia and Azerbaijan, the company said.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-expands-its-geographic-footprint-by-entering-the-azerbaijan-market/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-expands-its-geographic-footprint-by-entering-the-azerbaijan-market/ "ACWA Power expands its geographic footprint by entering the Azerbaijan market"

title: Solar Projects Show Rapid Growth in Middle East
content:
Solar power is expanding rapidly across the Middle East and North Africa, with the Middle East Solar Industry Association expecting at least $15 billion of solar projects to enter operation in the region over the next five years.

The regional solar market is worth more than $20 billion, according to MESIA estimates. Solar photovoltaic technology has become the most competitive form of power generation in the Gulf region, the International Renewable Energy Agency said in a report released earlier this year. Gulf Cooperation Council countries, including the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia, plan to install almost 7 GW of new renewable power generation capacity by the early 2020s.

Saudi Arabia advanced to the second phase of its solar procurement plan by opening bidding for seven projects totaling more than 1.5 GW of capacity. Oman has begun procurement for at least 1 GW of residential solar generation. Algeria announced tenders for 2 GW of solar generation.

The UAE leads the region with the largest operational and in-development solar projects, representing 58% of MENA's market share, followed by Egypt at 23%, Jordan at 10%, and Morocco at 6%, according to Mina Mesbahi, a research analyst at Solarplaza International B.V. Iran accounts for 3%.

Mesbahi told the Saudi Gazette that almost all countries in the region experience year-round sunshine. The region has approximately 3 GW of operational solar capacity.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/solar-projects-show-rapid-growth-in-middle-east/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/solar-projects-show-rapid-growth-in-middle-east/ "Solar Projects Show Rapid Growth in Middle East"

title: NATIONWIDE BOILER INC. SECURES LARGEST RENTAL PROJECT IN THEIR FIFTY-ONE YEAR HISTORY
content:
Nationwide Boiler Inc. secured its largest rental project in 51 years, supplying three high-capacity boilers for Saudi Aramco's Jizan Refinery project in Saudi Arabia.

The company will provide three 200,000 lb/hr package watertube boilers designed for 750 psi and 750F superheated steam, along with a plant master workstation and one deaerator tank with pumps. The equipment will remain on rental for a minimum of one year during the refinery's commissioning phase.

The boilers were sourced from Nationwide Boiler's existing inventory as part of its equipment stocking program, enabling quick delivery. William Testa, Director of Sales at Nationwide Boiler, said the company's immediate availability of the equipment was critical to winning the order. "A significant amount of preparation, multiple discussions to formulate an offering that fit their needs best, and a committed team with 'all hands-on deck' is really what sealed the deal," Testa stated.

Nationwide Boiler is a representative and stocking distributor of Babcock & Wilcox package watertube boilers. The company worked with Middle East representative Aujan Industrial Solutions and EPC customer Redland Industrial Services (Arabia) Ltd. to secure the project.

The refinery will be located across 106 square kilometers in Jizan and is expected to process more than 400,000 barrels per day of crude oil. The facility will be self-sufficient in power generation and will serve both domestic and international customers. The equipment will ship via roll-on/roll-off vessel from Houston, with an expected six-week transit time to the Port of Jizan.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/nationwide-boiler-inc-secures-largest-rental-project-in-their-fifty-one-year-history/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/nationwide-boiler-inc-secures-largest-rental-project-in-their-fifty-one-year-history/ "NATIONWIDE BOILER INC. SECURES LARGEST RENTAL PROJECT IN THEIR FIFTY-ONE YEAR HISTORY"

title: ACWA Power Wins First Utility-Scale Renewable Energy Project in Saudi Arabia
content:
ACWA Power won Saudi Arabia's first utility-scale renewable energy project, a 300 MW solar installation that will set a new world record tariff for photovoltaic power at 8.782 halala per kilowatt-hour, or 2.34 U.S. cents per kWh.

The Skaka project, located in Al Jouf on a site spanning more than six square kilometers, represents the opening phase of Saudi Arabia's ambitious national renewables program targeting 9.5 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2023. ACWA Power secured a 25-year power purchase contract for the facility, which carries an investment value of 993,750 million Saudi riyals, equivalent to $256 million.

Mohammad Abdullah Abunayyan, chairman of ACWA Power, said the project marks another world record tariff achievement for the company and underscores its selection as developer of the Kingdom's first utility-scale renewable plant. "We are humbled to be entrusted with the first utility-scale renewable energy plant to be developed in the Kingdom, which will be the foundation of the visionary ambitious national program," Abunayyan said.

The Saudi developer, which operates power generation and desalination facilities across 11 countries, brings experience from previous renewable energy projects in the United Arab Emirates, Jordan, Egypt, Morocco and South Africa. Abunayyan noted that winning Skaka allows the company to deploy its expertise and capabilities to ensure delivery at global quality and efficiency standards.

The Renewable Energy Projects Development Office managed the procurement process, which Abunayyan praised for maintaining standards of professionalism, transparency and integrity throughout prequalification and final selection.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-wins-first-utility-scale-renewable-energy-project-in-saudi-arabia/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-wins-first-utility-scale-renewable-energy-project-in-saudi-arabia/ "ACWA Power Wins First Utility-Scale Renewable Energy Project in Saudi Arabia"

title: Wärtsilä ensures the reliability and availability of Saudi Arabian power plant
content:
Wärtsilä signed a three-year asset management agreement in October 2017 to continue operating and maintaining the 62 MW power plant operated by Saudi Arabia-based Northern Region Cement Company in Turaif.

The agreement extends Wärtsilä's relationship with NRCC, which began in 2008. Under the contract, Wärtsilä is responsible for day-to-day operations, preventive maintenance and predictive maintenance of the facility. The company's Customer Centre in Dubai remotely monitors the plant's condition to provide technical advisory and data analysis services.

"Wärtsilä has been responsible for the operation and maintenance of our power plant already since 2008. We have been very satisfied with the quality of Wärtsilä's service and support as well as the way they ensure safe and reliable operations," said Dr. Obaid Alsobiei, CEO of Northern Region Cement Company.

Over the years, Wärtsilä has performed electrical and automation upgrades to improve performance and extend the plant's lifecycle. The company said its oversight has ensured reliable operations, optimized fuel consumption and reduced maintenance costs for NRCC.

"By having full responsibility for the operation and maintenance of NRCC's power plant, we have been able to ensure reliable operations, optimised fuel consumption and reduced maintenance costs for NRCC," said Haidar al Hertani, Managing Director of Wärtsilä Saudi Arabia.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-ensures-the-reliability-and-availability-of-saudi-arabian-power-plant/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-ensures-the-reliability-and-availability-of-saudi-arabian-power-plant/ "Wärtsilä ensures the reliability and availability of Saudi Arabian power plant"

title: Wärtsilä to supply 38 MW power plant to Saudi Arabia
content:
Wärtsilä has received an order to supply a 38 MW power plant to Saudi Arabia in the first quarter of 2017. The turn-key engineering, procurement and construction order includes four Wärtsilä 32TS engines, which are two-stage turbocharged units optimized for extreme ambient conditions.

The 32TS engine is designed to deliver high efficiency with reduced fuel and water consumption in high altitudes and hot temperatures while meeting World Bank environmental, health and safety guidelines for NOx emissions. This will be the first Wärtsilä 32TS installation to operate primarily on crude oil, with heavy and light fuel oils as backup fuels.

The project aligns with Saudi Arabia's recently launched energy sector transformation programme, which includes initiatives to improve energy efficiency and increase the role of gas and renewables in the energy mix. The project supports the Saudi Vision 2030 programme.

Qasim Latif, Business Development Manager at Wärtsilä Energy Solutions, said the company's solutions offer high efficiency, operational flexibility, multi-fuel capability and minimal water consumption, along with the ability to enable renewable energy integration.

Wärtsilä has operated in Saudi Arabia for more than 40 years. Upon completion of this project, the company will have more than 1,900 MW of installed capacity in the country. Globally, Wärtsilä's installed base exceeds 63 GW across 176 countries.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-to-supply-38-mw-power-plant-to-saudi-arabia/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-to-supply-38-mw-power-plant-to-saudi-arabia/ "Wärtsilä to supply 38 MW power plant to Saudi Arabia"

title: MAN Diesel & Turbo builds power plant in Saudi Arabia
content:
MAN Diesel & Turbo has secured an engineering, procurement and construction contract to build a 40 MW power plant for Arabian Cement Company in Rabigh, Saudi Arabia, around 120 km north of Jeddah.

The facility will be equipped with two type 18V48/60 TS engines and will operate on heavy fuel oil and diesel oil to supply power to a cement factory. The two-stage turbocharged engines offer higher efficiency and greater power density compared to single-turbocharger versions.

Construction of the first phase is scheduled for 2016/17. The plant's capacity will be expanded to 120 MW through two additional phases, which will involve installing four more engines.

"MAN Diesel & Turbo has been a reliable partner of ours for many years," said Gasem Al Maimani, President and CEO of Arabian Cement Company. "In addition to a high level of professionalism and the quality of the engines, we appreciate the company's flexibility and capacity for innovation."

As consortium leader, MAN Diesel & Turbo is responsible for constructing the entire facility. The company said it has extensive prior experience in the region. In 2016, MAN will also hand over a 55 MW power plant to United Cement Industrial Company, which will be operated by MAN's service brand PrimeServ.

Saudi Arabia represents a growing core market for MAN Diesel & Turbo's power plant division. "We have established a good reputation by completing a large number of successful projects in Saudi Arabia," said Alexander Stöckler, Global Head of Sales at MAN Diesel & Turbo's power plant division.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/man-diesel-turbo-builds-power-plant-in-saudi-arabia/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/man-diesel-turbo-builds-power-plant-in-saudi-arabia/ "MAN Diesel & Turbo builds power plant in Saudi Arabia"

title: EDF power solutions North America and Masdar Sign Agreements for Solar and Energy Storage Project
content:
EDF power solutions North America and Masdar have signed 15-year power purchase agreements for output from the BigBeau Solar+Storage Project in Kern County, California, with the facility beginning electricity deliveries to Southern California Edison on Feb. 1, 2026.

The project comprises a 128 megawatt (MWac) solar plant and a 40 MW/160 MWh battery energy storage system. BigBeau has been operational since December 2022, with the new agreements covering power sales to SCE going forward.

Dustin Priemer, Director Asset Management Masdar Americas, said the agreement reflects the company's focus on scaling reliable, utility-scale clean power. "We are appreciative of our growing partnership with Southern California Edison and our shared commitment to investing in new generation capacity to meet growing energy demand in California," Priemer said.

Jacqueline de Fresart, Associate Director of Origination and Power Marketing at EDF power solutions North America, added that the company is pleased to support SCE's clean energy goals. "We are excited to partner with SCE again and look forward to more opportunities together," de Fresart said.

BigBeau is one of seven projects that Masdar and EDF power solutions agreed to develop together, with combined capacity of 1.1 gigawatts (GW). EDF power solutions has developed 26 gigawatts of wind, solar and storage projects over more than 35 years. Masdar, active in the United States since 2019, has invested several billion dollars in the U.S. market and plans to develop a portfolio of up to 25 GW within the next decade.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/edf-power-solutions-north-america-and-masdar-sign-agreements-for-solar-and-energy-storage-project

title: RWE and Masdar receive planning permission for 3GW Dogger Bank South offshore wind farm projects
content:
RWE and Masdar have received planning permission for two 3GW offshore wind farms off the northeast coast of England, following approval by Lord Whitehead on behalf of the UK Energy Secretary.

The Dogger Bank South East and West projects, each with 1.5GW capacity, will be co-located over 100km off the coast and capable of generating enough electricity to power approximately three million UK homes annually. The joint venture between RWE (51%) and Masdar (49%) secured Contracts for Difference through the UK Government's Allocation Round 7 in January 2026.

The Development Consent Order was granted after a lengthy review process that began in June 2024. The Planning Inspectorate examined over 1,000 documents and conducted 10 online examination hearings before granting approval.

RWE and Masdar will now focus on detailed final design work and procurement decisions, with the aim of reaching final investment decision in 2027. The projects are expected to create opportunities for businesses and communities in Yorkshire, the Humber and wider UK.

The Dogger Bank South development adds to offshore wind capacity being developed by the partners in UK waters, as part of broader efforts to support the country's renewable energy targets.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/rwe-and-masdar-receive-planning-permission-for-3gw-dogger-bank-south-offshore-wind-farm-projects

title: Allocation Round 7 (AR7)
content:
The UK government awarded RWE a total of 6.9 gigawatts of offshore wind capacity through Contracts for Difference in Allocation Round 7, with a strike price of £91.20 per megawatt hour in 2024 prices, inflation-indexed.

RWE secured 20-year contracts for five projects: Norfolk Vanguard East and West, two Dogger Bank South projects and Awel y Môr. All projects are located in British waters, with four in the North Sea and one in the Irish Sea.

The company will jointly develop Norfolk Vanguard East and West with KKR, a global investment firm. KKR will acquire a 50% equity stake in each Norfolk Vanguard project, with both partners developing, constructing and operating the wind farms. The transaction is expected to close in summer 2026, subject to customary approvals.

Markus Krebber, CEO of RWE AG, said the company was "delighted to have been successful in securing long-term offtake contracts for five projects in AR7" and praised the partnership with KKR for combining "KKR's investment know-how in large-scale, complex infrastructure projects with RWE's extensive offshore wind expertise."

RWE is developing the Dogger Bank South projects with Masdar and Awel y Môr with Stadtwerke München and Siemens.

The allocation outcome marks a key milestone for RWE's UK offshore wind development pipeline, the company said.

Source: https://uk.rwe.com/press-and-news/2026-01-14-rwe-secures-contracts-for-difference-for-6-9-gigawatts-of-offshore-wind-capacity/

title: EWEC and Masdar Forge Strategic Framework to Rapidly Scale Renewable Energy and Drive National Decarbonisation Goals
content:
EWEC and Masdar signed a strategic collaboration agreement aimed at accelerating deployment of more than 30GW of solar photovoltaic capacity and over 8GW of battery storage in the UAE.

The Collaboration Framework Agreement, signed by Ahmed Ali Alshamsi, chief executive of EWEC, and Mohamed Jameel Al Ramahi, chief executive of Masdar, establishes a roadmap for utility-scale renewable energy projects to diversify the UAE's energy mix and strengthen supply security.

The agreement will streamline Masdar's participation in EWEC's engagement process for renewable energy projects, covering early-stage development through to financial close while preserving the transparency and independence of EWEC's competitive procurement processes.

EWEC, which handles integrated planning, purchasing and supply of water and electricity across the UAE, aims to meet 60% of Abu Dhabi's total energy demand from renewable and clean sources by 2035. The framework is designed to maximize in-country value and foster Emirati talent and expertise.

"This Collaboration Framework Agreement with Masdar is a pivotal strategic tool that empowers us to accelerate this transformation and meet 60 per cent of Abu Dhabi's total energy demand from renewable and clean sources by 2035," Alshamsi said.

The two companies have a history of partnership on projects including Al Dhafra Solar PV, Al Ajban Solar PV, Khazna Solar PV, and a gigascale solar and battery storage project. The agreement aims to improve efficiency while maintaining the transparency of EWEC's procurement processes.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/wec-and-masdar-forge-strategic-framework-to-scale-renewable-energy

title: Masdar and EPCG to form joint venture to enhance energy security in Montenegro, the Western Balkans and Southern Europe
content:
Abu Dhabi-based clean energy company Masdar and Elektroprivreda Crne Gore, Montenegro's state-owned power utility, have agreed to form a 50/50 joint venture to develop large-scale renewable energy projects in the country.

The joint venture, to be headquartered in Nikšić, will deploy and operate solar photovoltaic, wind, hydropower, pumped hydro energy storage, stand-alone battery energy storage systems and hybrid solutions across Montenegro. The agreement was signed at the Adria Future Summit in the presence of Montenegro's Minister of Energy and Mining Admir Šahmanović and the UAE's Ambassador to Montenegro.

Masdar CEO Mohamed Jameel Al Ramahi and EPCG CEO Zdravko Dragaš signed the deal alongside other representatives from both organizations.

The partnership aims to support Montenegro's domestic energy requirements while enabling the country to export renewable electricity to the Western Balkans and Southern Europe. The joint venture will leverage an existing sub-sea interconnection with Italy to facilitate electricity exports.

The collaboration combines EPCG's position as Montenegro's leading energy producer with Masdar's global expertise in renewable energy project development. Masdar already has an established presence in Montenegro, which the partnership builds upon.

Šahmanović said the partnership represents a turning point for Montenegro's energy future. "By joining forces, Masdar and EPCG bring together global knowledge, strong national capacity and a shared ambition to speed up the transition towards clean energy," he said.

The joint venture is intended to enhance Montenegro's energy security while accelerating the country's energy transformation and supporting the broader European energy market transition.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/masdar-and-epcg-to-form-joint-venture

title: ScottishPower and Masdar set UK record for biggest blade install at East Anglia THREE offshore windfarm
content:
ScottishPower and Masdar have installed the first turbine at their East Anglia THREE offshore windfarm, setting a UK record with 115-metre blades, the largest manufactured and deployed in the country.

The 1.4 GW project off the Suffolk coast will eventually comprise 95 turbines. Each of the 14MW Siemens Gamesa units stands approximately 262 metres tall with a rotor diameter of 236 metres. All 285 blades for the windfarm are manufactured in the UK at Siemens Gamesa's factory in Hull.

When fully operational, East Anglia THREE will generate sufficient power for more than 1.3 million homes. A single turbine revolution will produce enough electricity to power a UK household for over four days.

ScottishPower Renewables CEO Charlie Jordan said the project represents "a defining moment and a UK industry first" that will invest billions of pounds in UK and global supply chains, support thousands of construction jobs and create more than 100 long-term positions in East England.

"East Anglia THREE will be the biggest and most powerful offshore windfarm in our portfolio," Jordan said. "This achievement is testament to successful collaboration with our strategic investment partner, Masdar, and the dedication and excellence of our own teams and supply chain partners."

The £4 billion project is backed by ScottishPower, its parent company Iberdrola, and the Abu Dhabi-based Masdar. When complete, it will rank among the world's largest offshore windfarms, contributing to UK energy security and clean power targets.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/first-turbine-installed-at-east-anglia-three

title: https://wiser.ae/
content:
WiSER, a global platform established in 2015 by Masdar and the Zayed Sustainability Prize, operates three programs focused on advancing women as leaders in sustainable development. The organization has graduated more than 150 professional young women representing 30 or more nationalities through its year-long Pioneers Program, which targets women aged 25-35 with educational workshops, mentoring and networking opportunities with industry experts.

WiSER has convened over 4,000 influential figures to advance gender parity and sustainability initiatives. The platform has provided more than 565 hours of mentoring to young women pursuing sustainability leadership roles and conducted more than 540 hours of workshops with Pioneers participants.

The Pioneers Program equips participants with knowledge and skills to drive change toward a net-zero future. WiSER also operates the WiSER Cares cultural exchange program, which combines hands-on learning across five pillars including leadership, innovation, energy nexus, community engagement and global networking. A separate WiSER Mentorship Program pairs Pioneers alumni with senior industry mentors to support career development.

The organization is rooted in recognition that women, often disproportionately affected by environmental and development challenges, can serve as powerful agents of change. WiSER's work aligns with the United Arab Emirates' sustainability vision and the legacy of Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan. The platform announced its annual forum, "Igniting a Global Sustainable Economy," scheduled for January 1, 2025.

Source: https://wiser.ae/

title: TotalEnergies and Masdar to form $2.2 billion Joint Venture to Accelerate Renewable Energy Growth in Asia
content:
TotalEnergies and Abu Dhabi Future Energy Company (Masdar) signed a binding agreement to form a $2.2 billion 50/50 joint venture that will merge their onshore renewable energy operations across nine Asian countries.

The joint venture will operate in Azerbaijan, Indonesia, Japan, Kazakhstan, Malaysia, the Philippines, Singapore, South Korea and Uzbekistan, serving as the sole vehicle for both companies to develop, build, own and operate solar, wind and battery storage projects in these markets.

Each partner will contribute assets of comparable value to the venture. The combined portfolio will comprise 3 GW of operational capacity and 6 GW of projects in advanced development expected to reach operation by 2030.

The partnership responds to accelerating electricity demand growth across Asia. TotalEnergies has previously deployed renewable energy projects in Central Asia and the Caucasus and has an expanding portfolio in Asia-Pacific markets. Masdar, headquartered in Abu Dhabi, is a global clean energy company.

"Asia will be the main driver of global electricity demand growth this decade, and this collaboration with TotalEnergies will accelerate our progress across the continent," said His Excellency Dr. Sultan Al Jaber, UAE Minister of Industry and Advanced Technology and Chairman of Masdar.

The joint venture will combine the operational expertise and capital resources of both companies to develop renewable energy projects at scale in some of Asia's fastest-growing energy markets. The transaction remains subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/totalenergies-and-masdar

title: Exus Renewables to acquire stake in Masdar’s Portuguese wind portfolio
content:
Exus Renewables has agreed to acquire a 60 percent stake in Masdar's portfolio of nine wind farms in northern and central Portugal, with Masdar retaining a 40 percent stake.

The portfolio, located in Guarda and Castelo Branco, is undergoing repowering that will increase its operational capacity from 144 MW to 164 MW. Completion of the repowering project is expected by 2027. Once fully operational, the sites will generate electricity for more than 200,000 households and avoid approximately 41.7 kilotons of CO₂ emissions annually.

The companies also plan to codevelop 110 MW of solar capacity at the sites as part of a hybridization initiative, which would further enhance the portfolio's output.

Luis Adão da Fonseca, CEO at Exus Renewables, said repowering unlocks significant new renewable capacity from existing sites. "This deal significantly strengthens Exus' operational presence across Europe and underscores our innovative approach to maximizing the performance and value of renewable assets," he said.

Mohamed Jameel Al Ramahi, Chief Executive Officer of Masdar, said the initiative enhanced the performance and long-term value of the sites. "Disciplined portfolio management and partnership are fundamental aspects of our growth strategy," he said.

The Portuguese repowering initiative aligns with the country's energy transition roadmap, which targets 10.4 GW of onshore wind capacity by 2030 and achieving net zero by a specified date.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/exus-renewables-to-acquire-stake-in-masdars-portuguese-wind-portfolio

title: Power to Change film series
content:
Masdar is driving one of Central Asia's most ambitious renewable energy programs in Uzbekistan, according to a new documentary in the company's Power to Change film series released on November 13.

Since entering Uzbekistan in 2018, Masdar has committed over US$2 billion across major solar and wind developments. These include the 500MW Zarafshan Wind Farm, the 457MW Sherabad solar project and the 100MW Nur Navoi solar project.

The documentary captures Uzbekistan's transition from its industrial past towards a cleaner energy future. Ilholm Jon Teshabaev, General Director of Uzbekistan Masdar, said in the film: "Our relationship with power is a very old one. We know our history, our old industries and how we generated power. But the world is moving forward and so have we."

Abdulla Zayed, Director of Development and Investment at Masdar, stated: "From the beginning, our mission was to identify how Masdar could best support Uzbekistan's transition to a green and more sustainable energy system."

Masdar and the Government of Uzbekistan are advancing the country's first standalone Battery Energy Storage System, which will help balance the grid and stabilize supply. The projects are providing secure, affordable energy to communities across the country.

The Power to Change series highlights international cooperation in accelerating renewable energy development in the Central Asian nation.

Source: https://masdar.ae/news/newsroom?topicID={AF1B6ECC-3E90-4CAF-A036-6C76456D4A95}

title: Zayed Sustainability Prize
content:
Abu Dhabi Sustainability Week 2026 concluded as its largest edition, drawing over 60,000 attendees and 15 heads of state under the patronage of UAE President His Highness Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan.

The five-day event, held from January 11-15 under the theme "The Nexus of Next: All Systems Go," brought together over 400 ministers and government officials, more than double the previous year's number. Hosted by Masdar, a global clean energy company, the gathering featured more than 240 dedicated sessions with over 800 speakers.

Attendee numbers increased 20% compared to the 2025 edition, according to organizers. The event convened leaders from energy, finance, food, water and nature sectors to address sustainability challenges and systems transformation.

The week positioned Abu Dhabi Sustainability Week as the world's largest sustainability gathering and highlighted the UAE's clean energy leadership on the global stage. Discussions centered on global energy systems transformation, food and water security, and nature conservation.

Masdar organized the event to facilitate dialogue among government officials, business leaders and civil society representatives on pressing sustainability issues. The expanded attendance and number of sessions reflected growing international interest in coordinated approaches to environmental and energy challenges.

The 2026 edition demonstrated renewed commitment from participating nations to accelerate real-world sustainability solutions, according to organizers.

Source: https://masdar.ae/news/newsroom?topicID={4D4CF84D-930F-4B2C-9BE8-3584C1382126}

title: Acwa Recognized as a Global Leader in Water at the 2026 Global Water Awards in Madrid
content:
Acwa has been named Desalination Company of the Year and Desalination Plant of the Year at the 2026 Global Water Awards in Madrid, the company said on June 3.

The awards recognize Acwa as the world's largest private water desalination company, managing 9.7 million m³/day globally. The company operates 109 assets across operation, advanced development, or under construction stages.

The Global Water Awards, organized by Global Water Intelligence since 2006, recognize excellence across the international water sector across 15 categories spanning desalination, wastewater treatment, digital innovation and sustainable financing.

Acwa's flagship project, Rabigh 3, a 600,000 m³/day seawater reverse osmosis plant serving the Makkah region, has delivered availability levels above 99%, the company said. The project recently completed a refinancing aligned with IFC Blue Finance Guidelines.

Dr. Tariq Nada, Head of Technical Services, Global Water at Acwa, said the award "is a testament to the engineers, operators and partners across our entire ecosystem." He added the company is "proud to provide a significant share of Saudi Arabia's desalinated water capacity."

Yasir Mahmoud, Executive Vice President of Global Water at Acwa, said the dual award recognitions signal "that our model works with lower energy intensity, higher availability and smarter financing." He added the company has focused on making reverse osmosis "not only affordable, but also cleaner, more reliable, and ready to scale into new markets."

The company describes itself as a leader in the energy transition and a first mover into green hydrogen at scale.

Source: https://acwapower.com/en/media-center/latest-news/acwa-recognized-as-a-global-leader-in-water-at-the-2026-global-water-awards-in-madrid/

title: Acwa, BCDA Advance Philippines' Clean Energy Transition with 500-MW Solar-Plus-Storage Project in New Clark City
content:
Acwa Power Philippines signed a 500-hectare contract of lease with the Bases Conversion and Development Authority for an integrated solar and battery storage facility in New Clark City, the Philippines' flagship smart city development.

The project will feature utility-scale solar photovoltaic and battery energy storage systems with capacity of up to 500 MW upon full build-out, subject to final design and regulatory approvals. Acwa Power Philippines plans an initial investment of at least USD 400,000 per megawatt in the facility, which will power data centers and advanced manufacturing operations in the region.

Finance Secretary Frederick D. Go said the investment "lays the foundation upon which semiconductors, advanced manufacturing, and AI-driven industries can be built at utility scale, while creating meaningful, lasting jobs for Filipinos."

The project represents a key investment commitment secured by the Philippine government under President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr. to support sustainable economic growth and energy security objectives.

Acwa Power Chief Executive Officer Dr. Samir J Serhan said the partnership demonstrates investor confidence in the Philippines for large-scale clean infrastructure development. "New Clark City reflects the kind of integrated, future-ready development that global energy investors are looking for," he said.

Salman M. Baray, Country General Manager of Acwa Philippines, said the lease footprint allows the company to build an integrated project rather than an incremental one. The company will also invest in host communities around the site, he added.

Acwa Power is the world's largest private water desalination company and a leader in energy transition and green hydrogen at scale.

Source: https://acwapower.com/en/media-center/latest-news/acwa-bcda-advance-philippines-clean-energy-transition-with-500-mw-solar-plus-storage-project-in-new-clark-city/

title: Acwa and Emerging Power Inc. Sign Joint Development Agreement (JDA) to Build up to 5,000 MW Clean Energy Platform by 2030 in the Philippines
content:
Acwa Power Philippines and Emerging Power Inc. signed a Joint Development Agreement to jointly pursue up to 5,000 MW of renewable energy and gas-fired generation capacity across the Philippines by 2030.

The two companies will identify and co-develop new power projects nationwide, with an initial focus on joint participation in the Philippines' Green Energy Auction Program (GEAP). Emerging Power is the clean energy arm of Nickel Asia Corporation, one of Southeast Asia's largest lateritic nickel ore producers.

"The Philippines has set one of the most ambitious clean energy trajectories in Southeast Asia, and reaching it requires partners who can move at scale and at pace," said Dr. Samir J Serhan, Chief Executive Officer of Acwa. "NAC brings deep local knowledge, an operating renewables base through EPI. We bring three decades of delivering large-scale power and water assets on time and on budget."

Martin Antonio G. Zamora, Chairman and CEO of EPI, said the partnership "actively accelerates the nation's transition to a low-carbon economy. Together, we are building scalable, reliable, and sustainable power infrastructure that will energize Filipino communities, drive economic growth."

Salman M. Baray, Country General Manager of Acwa Philippines, described the agreement as combining local execution capability with global development expertise. "GEAP is the obvious first test, but the real value is the longer pipeline we can shape together," he said.

Acwa is a Saudi-listed company that operates water desalination and power generation assets globally. The agreement comes as the Philippines accelerates its power sector development to meet rising electrification demand.

Source: https://acwapower.com/en/media-center/latest-news/acwa-and-emerging-power-inc-sign-joint-development-agreement-jda-to-build-up-to-5-000-mw-clean-energy-platform-by-2030-in-the-philippines/

title: EDF power solutions North America and Masdar Sign Agreements for Solar and Energy Storage Project
content:
EDF power solutions North America and Masdar have signed 15-year power purchase agreements covering electricity output from the BigBeau Solar+Storage Project in Kern County, California, which began supplying Southern California Edison on February 1, 2026.

The project comprises a 128 megawatt solar plant and a 40 MW/160 MWh battery energy storage system. BigBeau has been operational since December 2022 under co-ownership by EDF power solutions North America and Abu Dhabi Future Energy Company, Masdar.

Dustin Priemer, Director Asset Management at Masdar Americas, said the agreement reflects the company's focus on scaling reliable, utility-scale clean power in the United States. "We are appreciative of our growing partnership with Southern California Edison and our shared commitment to investing in new generation capacity to meet growing energy demand in California," Priemer said.

Jacqueline de Fresart, Associate Director of Origination and Power Marketing at EDF power solutions North America, stated the company was pleased to support SCE's clean energy goals. "We are excited to partner with SCE again and look forward to more opportunities together," de Fresart said.

BigBeau is one of seven projects Masdar and EDF power solutions agreed to develop jointly, with combined capacity of 1.1 gigawatts. EDF power solutions has developed 26 gigawatts of wind, solar and storage projects across North America over 35 years. Masdar, active in the United States since 2019, has invested several billion dollars and plans to develop a portfolio of up to 25 GW within the next decade.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/edf-power-solutions-north-america-and-masdar-sign-agreements-for-solar-and-energy-storage-project

title: RWE and Masdar receive planning permission for 3GW Dogger Bank South offshore wind farm projects
content:
RWE and Masdar received planning permission to build two offshore wind farms with a combined capacity of 3 gigawatts off the northeast coast of England. Lord Whitehead, acting on behalf of the UK Energy Secretary, approved the Development Consent Order for Dogger Bank South West and Dogger Bank South East on May 14.

The projects, each generating 1.5 gigawatts, will be located over 100 kilometers off the Yorkshire and Humber coast. The joint venture is 51% owned by RWE and 49% owned by Abu Dhabi Future Energy Company PJSC, known as Masdar. The wind farms are expected to generate enough electricity to power approximately three million UK homes annually.

Both projects secured Contracts for Difference through the UK Government's Allocation Round 7 in January 2026. The Development Consent Order application was submitted in June 2024 and underwent 10 online examination hearings before the Planning Inspectorate.

RWE and Masdar will now undertake detailed final design work and procurement decisions, with a target to make a final investment decision in 2027. The companies identified Dogger Bank South as part of their wider clean power portfolio in the United Kingdom.

The approval marks a significant milestone for the developers as the projects advance toward construction. Both wind farms will operate from the same location, allowing for shared infrastructure and operational synergies.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/rwe-and-masdar-receive-planning-permission-for-3gw-dogger-bank-south-offshore-wind-farm-projects

title: Allocation Round 7 (AR7)
content:
The UK government has awarded RWE Contracts for Difference for 6.9 gigawatts of offshore wind capacity in the Allocation Round 7 auction, securing a strike price of £91.20 per megawatt hour in 2024 prices, inflation-indexed.

RWE's four projects that secured 20-year contracts are Norfolk Vanguard East and Norfolk Vanguard West in the British North Sea, two Dogger Bank South projects, also in the North Sea, and Awel y Môr in the Irish Sea, the UK Department for Energy Security and Net Zero confirmed on January 14.

The company has entered into a partnership with investment firm KKR to develop, construct and operate the Norfolk Vanguard East and Norfolk Vanguard West projects. KKR will acquire a 50% equity stake in each project, with both partners sharing responsibility for delivery. The transaction is expected to close in summer 2026.

"We are delighted to have been successful in securing long-term offtake contracts for five projects in AR7," RWE AG Chief Executive Markus Krebber said. "By combining KKR's investment know-how in large-scale, complex infrastructure projects with RWE's extensive offshore wind expertise, we are well positioned to jointly realise these major projects."

RWE is developing the Dogger Bank South projects with Masdar and the Awel y Môr project with Stadtwerke München and Siemens.

Source: https://uk.rwe.com/press-and-news/2026-01-14-rwe-secures-contracts-for-difference-for-6-9-gigawatts-of-offshore-wind-capacity/
