title: ScottishPower and Masdar set UK record for biggest blade install at East Anglia THREE offshore windfarm
content:
ScottishPower and Masdar installed the first turbine at East Anglia THREE offshore windfarm off the Suffolk coast, setting a UK record with 115-metre blades, the largest manufactured and deployed in the country.

All 285 blades for the project's 95 turbines are being produced at Siemens Gamesa's factory in Hull. The 14MW turbines stand approximately 262 metres tall with a rotor diameter of 236 metres. A single rotation of one turbine generates enough electricity to power a UK home for more than four days.

East Anglia THREE is a 1.4 GW project jointly developed by ScottishPower, Iberdrola and Masdar. When complete, the windfarm will produce sufficient clean power for more than 1.3 million homes.

Charlie Jordan, ScottishPower Renewables CEO, said the project represents "a defining moment and a UK industry first" that will invest billions of pounds in UK and global supply chains while creating thousands of jobs during construction and more than one hundred long-term roles in East England.

"East Anglia THREE will be the biggest and most powerful offshore windfarm in our portfolio," Jordan said. "This achievement is testament to successful collaboration with our strategic investment partner, Masdar, and the dedication and excellence of our own teams and supply chain partners."

The £4 billion project will be among the world's largest offshore windfarms when operational, contributing to UK energy security and clean energy targets.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/first-turbine-installed-at-east-anglia-three

title: https://wiser.ae/
content:
WiSER, a global platform established in 2015 by Masdar and the Zayed Sustainability Prize, operates programs designed to develop women as leaders in the sustainable energy transition. The platform works across three pillars: education, engagement and empowerment.

The WiSER Pioneers Program, a year-long initiative for women aged 25-35, has graduated more than 150 professional young women representing over 30 nationalities. Participants receive educational workshops, mentoring and networking opportunities with industry experts. The program has delivered 565 hours of mentoring and conducted 540 hours of workshops.

WiSER has convened over 4,000 influential figures to advance gender parity and sustainability efforts globally. The platform also operates the WiSER Cares cultural exchange program, which combines hands-on learning across five pillars: leadership, innovation, energy nexus, community engagement and global network. A separate WiSER Mentorship Program pairs graduates with senior industry mentors for career development and leadership growth.

The platform is rooted in the recognition that women, often disproportionately affected by environmental and development challenges, can be powerful agents of sustainable change. WiSER's work aligns with the UAE's sustainability vision and the legacy of Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan. The organization hosts an annual forum focused on igniting a global sustainable economy.

Source: https://wiser.ae/

title: TotalEnergies and Masdar to form $2.2 billion Joint Venture to Accelerate Renewable Energy Growth in Asia
content:
TotalEnergies and Abu Dhabi-based Masdar have signed a binding agreement to form a $2.2 billion 50/50 joint venture merging their onshore renewable energy activities across nine Asian countries.

The partnership will operate in Azerbaijan, Indonesia, Japan, Kazakhstan, Malaysia, the Philippines, Singapore, South Korea and Uzbekistan, serving as the sole vehicle for both companies to develop, build, own and operate solar, wind and battery storage projects in the region.

Each company will contribute assets of comparable value to the venture. The combined portfolio will include 3 GW of operational capacity and 6 GW under advanced development, with projects expected to reach completion by 2030.

Masdar Chairman Sultan Al Jaber said the partnership reflects the UAE's approach of "delivering at scale, investing with conviction, and building partnerships that endure." He noted that Asia will drive global electricity demand growth this decade, with the collaboration expected to unlock opportunities across the continent.

TotalEnergies has pioneered renewable energy deployment in Central Asia and the Caucasus and maintains an expanding portfolio in Asia-Pacific markets. Masdar has positioned itself as a global clean energy leader, with the joint venture aimed at capitalizing on the region's accelerating electricity demand.

The transaction is expected to close once standard regulatory approvals are obtained. Both companies said the venture would combine their strengths to secure significant positions in these high-growth markets.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/totalenergies-and-masdar

title: Exus Renewables to acquire stake in Masdar’s Portuguese wind portfolio
content:
Exus Renewables has agreed to acquire a 60% stake in a portfolio of nine wind farms in Portugal from Masdar, with the Abu Dhabi-based energy company retaining 40%.

The wind farms, located in Guarda and Castelo Branco in northern and central Portugal, are undergoing repowering to increase their combined operational capacity from 144MW to 164MW. Masdar and Exus plan to complete the repowering work by 2027.

Once fully operational, the repowered sites will supply electricity to more than 200,000 households and avoid approximately 41.7 kilotons of CO2 emissions annually, according to the companies.

The portfolio will also be hybridized with 110MW of additional solar capacity to be codeveloped by both companies. This solar addition would further boost the portfolio's output and help Portugal meet its clean energy targets.

Luis Adão da Fonseca, CEO at Exus Renewables, said the deal strengthens the company's presence across Europe and demonstrates its strategy to maximize performance from renewable assets. "Repowering unlocks significant new renewable capacity from existing sites," he said.

Mohamed Jameel Al Ramahi, Masdar's Chief Executive Officer, said the initiative reflects the company's approach to enhancing asset performance through repowering and hybridization. "Disciplined portfolio management and partnership are fundamental aspects of our growth strategy," he said.

The agreement aligns with Portugal's energy transition roadmap, which targets 10.4 GW of onshore wind capacity by 2030.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/exus-renewables-to-acquire-stake-in-masdars-portuguese-wind-portfolio

title: Power to Change film series
content:
Masdar is driving one of Central Asia's most ambitious renewable energy programs in Uzbekistan, according to a documentary released by the Abu Dhabi-based clean energy company.

Since entering Uzbekistan in 2018, Masdar has invested more than US$2 billion in major solar and wind developments, including the 500MW Zarafshan Wind Farm, the 457MW Sherabad solar project and the 100MW Nur Navoi solar facility.

"Our relationship with power is a very old one," said Ilholm Jon Teshabaev, General Director of Uzbekistan Masdar, in the film. "We know our history, our old industries and how we generated power. But the world is moving forward and so have we."

Masdar and the Government of Uzbekistan are now advancing the country's first standalone Battery Energy Storage System, the company said. The facility will help balance the grid, stabilize supply and ensure reliable clean power around the clock.

"From the beginning, our mission was to identify how Masdar could best support Uzbekistan's transition to a green and more sustainable energy system," said Abdulla Zayed, Director of Development and Investment at Masdar.

The documentary, part of Masdar's Power to Change film series, captures the transition of Uzbekistan from its industrial past towards a cleaner, more sustainable future. International cooperation plays a role in accelerating the country's clean energy progress, the company said.

Source: https://masdar.ae/news/newsroom?topicID={AF1B6ECC-3E90-4CAF-A036-6C76456D4A95}

title: Zayed Sustainability Prize
content:
Abu Dhabi Sustainability Week 2026 concluded as the world's largest sustainability gathering, attracting over 60,000 attendees and featuring 15 heads of state and more than 400 government ministers, the event organizers said on January 22.

The week-long event, held under the theme "The Nexus of Next: All Systems Go," drew 20% more participants than the previous year's edition. It featured more than 240 dedicated sessions with over 800 speakers addressing global energy systems transformation, food security, water security and nature conservation.

The event was held under the patronage of His Highness Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, President of the United Arab Emirates. Masdar, a global clean energy leader, hosted the gathering from January 11-15 in Abu Dhabi.

The week focused on how global systems across energy, finance, food, water and nature interconnect, scale and reinforce one another. Attendance more than doubled from the previous year, with the 2026 edition consolidating Abu Dhabi Sustainability Week's position as the world's largest sustainability event.

The gathering brought together business leaders, government officials and civil society representatives to advance dialogue on sustainability challenges and deliver systems transformation.

Source: https://masdar.ae/news/newsroom?topicID={4D4CF84D-930F-4B2C-9BE8-3584C1382126}

title: Data Center POWER eXchange
content:
Data Center POWER eXchange, a conference focused on the intersection of power generation and data center infrastructure, will hold its 2026 edition September 30 through October 1 in Washington, D.C.

The event brings together utilities, data center operators, developers, policymakers and investors to address electricity supply challenges driven by expanding artificial intelligence and digital infrastructure demands. Washington, D.C. hosts one of the fastest-growing data center regions in the United States, making it a focal point for these discussions.

The conference program addresses three core areas: grid interconnection, transmission and system capacity; on-site power generation, energy mix and reliability strategies; and scaling infrastructure to meet accelerating demand.

POWER Magazine, which has covered the power generation industry for more than a century, created the conference. The event takes place at the Omni Shoreham hotel.

Data Center POWER eXchange 2025 occurred earlier this year, with organizers releasing a recap video documenting discussions on power infrastructure and data center expansion.

The conference registration, sponsorship and hotel booking are available through the event website.

Source: https://www.datacenterpowerexchange.com/

title: Saudi Arabia Innovates to Support Renewable Energy Goals
content:
Saudi Arabia is pursuing advanced cooling technologies for high-efficiency solar cells as part of its renewable energy expansion strategy, according to research aligned with the country's Vision 2030 framework.

Triple-junction photovoltaic cells, which offer superior energy conversion capabilities, face operational challenges in high-temperature environments including thermal degradation and reduced performance. Effective thermal management is critical to maintaining efficiency and durability in these advanced systems.

Saudi Arabia has set a target to generate 50% of its electricity from renewable sources by 2030. The country plans to develop 130 GW of renewable energy capacity, comprising approximately 58.7 GW from solar energy and 40 GW from wind energy.

The nation's arid climate presents particular challenges for solar installations, as high ambient temperatures can significantly reduce photovoltaic performance. Active cooling systems integrated into solar installations can mitigate these effects, enabling systems to operate closer to optimal efficiency levels in the region's intense solar radiation environment.

Integration of advanced cooling technologies supports Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 objectives by enhancing solar generation efficiency and reliability while fostering innovation in clean energy solutions. The country aims to reduce dependence on oil revenues, diversify its economy, and develop a knowledge-based renewable energy sector.

By investing in efficient cooling solutions and advanced solar technologies, Saudi Arabia seeks to position itself as a leader in renewable energy innovation while meeting its electricity generation targets from renewable sources.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/saudi-arabia-innovates-to-support-renewable-energy-goals/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/saudi-arabia-innovates-to-support-renewable-energy-goals/ "Saudi Arabia Innovates to Support Renewable Energy Goals"

title: Carbon Capture Projects Come Online in Iceland, Saudi Arabia
content:
Carbon capture projects have come online in Iceland and Saudi Arabia as nations pursue pathways to reduce industrial emissions.

Iceland inaugurated Steingerdur, a carbon capture installation integrated into ON Power's geothermal plant that is expected to deliver about 10% of Iceland's 2030 Climate Action Plan targets for the energy and industrial sectors. The facility uses direct water capture to remove carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide emissions from the geothermal steam cycle, then applies Carbfix technology to store captured minerals underground where they turn into rock in approximately two years.

Iceland also operates Orca, a direct air capture and storage plant run by Swiss company Climeworks that began operations in September 2021.

In Saudi Arabia, Climeworks partnered with the King Abdullah Petroleum Studies and Research Center and the Saudi Energy Ministry to inaugurate a direct air capture testing unit at KAPSARC's facilities in Riyadh. The mobile unit captures CO2 directly from the atmosphere and validates Climeworks' technology under the region's hot and arid conditions.

ON Power and Carbfix, both part of Reykjavík Energy, operate the Steingerdur project. Climeworks, which established a U.S. headquarters in Austin, Texas last year, operates the Saudi demonstration facility.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/carbon-capture-projects-come-online-in-iceland-saudi-arabia/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/carbon-capture-projects-come-online-in-iceland-saudi-arabia/ "Carbon Capture Projects Come Online in Iceland, Saudi Arabia"

title: Aramco, Rondo Energy Studying GW-Scale Thermal Storage, Hydrogen, Carbon Capture Deployment
content:
Saudi Arabia's Aramco and Rondo Energy have signed a memorandum of understanding to conduct engineering studies for deploying thermal energy storage, hydrogen production and carbon capture technologies at Aramco facilities worldwide.

The two companies executed the agreement on May 16, with the initial project targeting industrial-scale deployment of Rondo's heat batteries across Aramco operations to reduce operating costs and support the Saudi energy giant's carbon emissions reduction goals. The project could scale to 1 GWh, with potential for additional deployment across Aramco's global facilities.

Aramco and Rondo also plan to explore joint development of novel applications for Rondo's Heat Battery technology, specifically in hydrogen production and carbon capture. Rondo's technology stores energy from wind and solar power, delivering continuous supply at gigawatt scale and high temperatures required for industrial processes.

Ali Al-Meshari, Aramco's senior vice president of Technology, Oversight and Coordination, said the company targets net-zero Scope 1 and Scope 2 operational greenhouse gas emissions by 2050 across wholly owned and operated assets. "Innovative technologies deployed at scale are needed to reduce the costs of emissions reduction," Al-Meshari said.

Aramco Ventures completed an equity investment in California-based Rondo Energy in July 2023. The investment underscores Aramco's focus on developing lower-carbon new energy businesses through research and development, venture capital and technology deployment programs.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/aramco-rondo-energy-studying-gw-scale-thermal-storage-hydrogen-carbon-capture-deployment/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/aramco-rondo-energy-studying-gw-scale-thermal-storage-hydrogen-carbon-capture-deployment/ "Aramco, Rondo Energy Studying GW-Scale Thermal Storage, Hydrogen, Carbon Capture Deployment"

title: Saudi Group Set to Build 10-GW Wind Farm in Egypt
content:
ACWA Power, a Saudi Arabian energy company, signed a memorandum of understanding on July 17 to build a 10-GW wind farm in Egypt, marking the next phase of a project first agreed in principle nearly two years ago.

The agreement between ACWA Power and Egypt's New and Renewable Energy Authority allocates about 3,000 square kilometers of land west of Sohag, on the west bank of the Nile River, for the installation. An initial accord between ACWA Power, the Egyptian Electricity Transmission Co., and the NREA was signed in November 2022.

Egypt's Minister of Electricity and Renewable Energy H.E. Dr. Mohamed Shaker Al-Marqabi said the project aligns with Egypt's goal to increase renewable energy capacity in the electricity mix to 42% by 2035, supporting the country's Vision 2030 and National Climate Strategy 2050.

"This project is also an extension of the historical relations between the Egyptian state and the brotherly Kingdom of Saudi Arabia," Al-Marqabi said in a statement.

Officials said the wind facility is expected to save Egypt approximately $6.5 billion annually in energy costs. The country is working to reduce its dependence on natural gas for power generation and achieve energy independence from fossil fuels while lowering carbon emissions.

ACWA Power is the world's largest private water desalination company. The project represents a major investment in Egypt's renewable energy capacity as the nation seeks to increase clean power generation for domestic consumption and export.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/saudi-group-set-to-build-10-gw-wind-farm-in-egypt/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/saudi-group-set-to-build-10-gw-wind-farm-in-egypt/ "Saudi Group Set to Build 10-GW Wind Farm in Egypt"

title: China, Saudi Arabia Team on Wind Projects in Uzbekistan
content:
China's Envision Energy will supply 1 GW of wind generation capacity for projects designed by Saudi Arabia's ACWA Power in Uzbekistan, the Shanghai-based turbine manufacturer announced on June 20.

Envision, China's second-largest wind turbine maker, will deliver its EN171-6.5 turbines to ACWA's Bash and Dzhankeldy onshore wind farms in Uzbekistan. The deal follows several other major turbine contracts for Envision in Egypt, India and Saudi Arabia in recent months.

The announcement comes after Envision was selected to supply approximately 1.7 GW of turbines for the $8.4 billion Neom hydrogen facility in Saudi Arabia. That project will integrate 4 GW of solar, wind and battery energy storage to produce up to 600 tonnes daily of green ammonia using more than 2 GW of hydrogen electrolysis capacity.

BloombergNEF ranked Envision the world's fourth-largest turbine supplier last year with 8.3 GW of installed generation capacity.

Uzbekistan has set a target of installing at least 5 GW of onshore wind by 2030 as it accelerates renewable energy deployment. ACWA Power signed a $10 billion investment cooperation agreement with Uzbekistan's Ministry of Energy in 2023 covering renewable energy, green hydrogen and gas-to-power projects over the next five years.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/china-saudi-arabia-team-on-wind-projects-in-uzbekistan/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/china-saudi-arabia-team-on-wind-projects-in-uzbekistan/ "China, Saudi Arabia Team on Wind Projects in Uzbekistan"

title: ACWA Power Inaugurates Renewable Energy Project
content:
ACWA Power inaugurated the 300 MW Sakaka photovoltaic project in Al Jouf, Saudi Arabia, marking the kingdom's first utility-scale renewable energy facility with a total investment of 1.2 billion Saudi riyals.

The ceremony took place under the patronage of Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud. Saudi Minister of Energy Prince Abdulaziz Bin Salman Bin Abdulaziz Al Saud and Prince Faisal bin Nawaf bin Abdulaziz, Governor of Al-Jouf Region, attended the event alongside senior government officials.

Prince Abdulaziz highlighted the private sector's role in renewable energy development, noting that the Public Investment Fund holds 50% ownership of ACWA Power. He stated that 97% of the operating team are Saudis, with 90% of the workforce from Al Jouf region.

ACWA Power, a leading Saudi developer and operator of power generation and water desalination plants, led the consortium that won the project. The facility was awarded at a world-record tariff of 8.781 halalas per kilowatt-hour in 2018, establishing a benchmark for subsequent renewable projects under Saudi Arabia's National Renewable Energy Program.

The Sakaka project represents a key component of the kingdom's energy diversification strategy, demonstrating cost reductions in clean solar electricity generation. The project's development involved collaboration with various Saudi companies.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-inaugurates-renewable-energy-project/

title: Doosan Škoda Power successfully launched two 200 MW turbines at the Fadhili power plant in Saudia Arabia
content:
Doosan Škoda Power has launched two 200 MW turbines at the Fadhili steam-gas power plant near Jubail in Saudi Arabia, with the units operating since March 2020.

The pair of DST-S10 condensation turbines will produce 418 MW of the Fadhili plant's total 1,509 MW capacity. The facility is owned jointly by Saudi Aramco, Saudi Electricity Company and French energy company Engie. Of the plant's output, 1,100 MW will supply electricity to local industry and residents, while 400 MW will serve the power plant's internal needs and gas extraction operations.

Doosan Škoda Power supplied the turbines along with pipelines, control systems, steam compression and drainage systems, oil systems and high-pressure hydraulic equipment. The Czech manufacturer worked with EPC supplier Doosan Heavy Industries & Construction Co. to complete the project.

Project manager Jindřich Závodný said the installation went smoothly due to cooperation within the group. "The project was executed for an electrical network operating under a different standard at a frequency of 60 Hz that required compliance with local standards and seismic codes," Závodný said in a statement. "The installation and commissioning were supervised by our experienced technical advisers, who, together with their Korean colleagues, handed over the units for commercial operation."

The equipment is currently under warranty, during which Doosan Škoda Power will provide support to ensure reliable operations. This marks the Czech manufacturer's first installation in Saudi Arabia.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/doosan-skoda-power-successfully-launched-two-200-mw-turbines-at-the-fadhili-power-plant-in-saudia-arabia/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/doosan-skoda-power-successfully-launched-two-200-mw-turbines-at-the-fadhili-power-plant-in-saudia-arabia/ "Doosan Škoda Power successfully launched two 200 MW turbines at the Fadhili power plant in Saudia Arabia"

title: Wärtsilä engine technology will enable Saudi Arabia mining site power plant to efficiently utilize solar energy
content:
Wärtsilä will supply engineering, equipment and technical advisory for a 44 MW power plant at the Mansourah & Massarah Gold Project mine in Saudi Arabia, marking the country's first newbuild power project using a hybrid concept combining engine technology with solar energy.

Larsen & Toubro Ltd, the India-based main contractors, placed the order with Wärtsilä in March. The power plant will comprise six Wärtsilä 32 engines, with five in operation and one on constant standby.

The plant's design capitalizes on the rapid response capability of Wärtsilä engines, which can reach full power output within minutes of startup. This flexibility enables efficient integration of variable renewable solar energy by allowing the system to respond quickly to fluctuations in power supply and balance electrical loads effectively.

"Reliability is key for any gold mine for securing uninterrupted operations and targeted revenues in all occasions," Amit Swarnkar, MENA BU Head of Metallurgical and Material Handling Division at Larsen & Turbo Ltd, said in a statement. "We have selected the Wärtsilä technology because of its capability to enable the integration of PV solar into the system."

Alexandre Eykerman, Energy Business Director for the Middle East at Wärtsilä, added that maximizing renewable energy use is central to the company's approach to enabling sustainable energy supplies.

Wärtsilä will deliver the equipment on a fast-track basis within 10 months from contract signing. The plant is scheduled for commissioning by May 2021, with commercial operations beginning a year later.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-engine-technology-will-enable-saudi-arabia-mining-site-power-plant-to-efficiently-utilize-solar-energy/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-engine-technology-will-enable-saudi-arabia-mining-site-power-plant-to-efficiently-utilize-solar-energy/ "Wärtsilä engine technology will enable Saudi Arabia mining site power plant to efficiently utilize solar energy"

title: ACWA Power expands its geographic footprint by entering the Azerbaijan market
content:
ACWA Power signed an implementation agreement with Azerbaijan's Ministry of Energy to develop, build and operate a 240 MW wind power project on a Build Own Operate basis with a 20-year power purchase agreement.

The ceremony took place in Baku on January 9, 2020, in the presence of Azerbaijani Prime Minister Ali Asadov and Energy Minister Parviz Shahbazov, alongside ACWA Power Chairman Mohammad Abunayyan and Chief Investment Officer Rajit Nanda.

The project represents one of the first two independent power projects in Azerbaijan and supports the government's efforts to diversify its energy mix away from oil and gas dependence. Azerbaijan possesses significant renewable energy resources that the government is seeking to leverage as part of major energy sector reforms.

"We are pleased to sign this implementation agreement with the Government of Azerbaijan, which expands our geographic footprint of operations into this culturally rich and economically vibrant country straddling Europe and Asia," said Paddy Padmanathan, Chief Executive Officer of ACWA Power.

Padmanathan added that the investment "reinforces the pivotal role that ACWA Power is committed to playing in deploying Saudi foreign investment to promote the decarbonisation of the electricity generation sector by supporting the ambitious sustainability plans and programs being pursued by an increasing number of countries."

ACWA Power is a Saudi Arabian company. The company said its expertise would help reduce renewable energy generation costs and provide affordable access to electricity for Azerbaijani communities while boosting the country's overall economic landscape.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-expands-its-geographic-footprint-by-entering-the-azerbaijan-market/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-expands-its-geographic-footprint-by-entering-the-azerbaijan-market/ "ACWA Power expands its geographic footprint by entering the Azerbaijan market"

title: Solar Projects Show Rapid Growth in Middle East
content:
Solar power installations are accelerating across the Middle East and North Africa, with the Middle East Solar Industry Association expecting at least $15 billion in solar projects to become operational over the next five years.

The region's solar market is valued at more than $20 billion, with photovoltaic technology now the most cost-competitive form of power generation in the Gulf region, according to the International Renewable Energy Agency. Gulf Cooperation Council countries, including the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia, plan to install nearly 7 GW of new renewable energy capacity by the early 2020s.

Saudi Arabia has advanced to a second phase of its solar procurement plan, opening bidding for seven projects totaling more than 1.5 GW of capacity. Oman has begun procurement for at least 1 GW of residential solar generation. Algeria announced tenders for 2 GW of solar generation.

The UAE leads the region with the largest operational and in-development solar projects, accounting for 58% of capacity in the top five MENA markets. Egypt follows with 23%, Jordan with 10%, Morocco with 6%, and Iran with 3%.

Mina Mesbahi, research analyst at Solarplaza International B.V., said the region benefits from year-round sunshine across most countries. The MENA region currently has approximately 3 GW of operational solar capacity.

"Almost all the countries in this region experience an all year-round sunshine, providing breeding grounds for solar development," Mesbahi told the Saudi Gazette.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/solar-projects-show-rapid-growth-in-middle-east/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/solar-projects-show-rapid-growth-in-middle-east/ "Solar Projects Show Rapid Growth in Middle East"

title: NATIONWIDE BOILER INC. SECURES LARGEST RENTAL PROJECT IN THEIR FIFTY-ONE YEAR HISTORY
content:
Nationwide Boiler Inc. secured its largest rental project in 51 years, supplying three 200,000 lb/hr package watertube boilers to Saudi Aramco's new Jizan Refinery in Saudi Arabia.

The boilers, manufactured by Babcock & Wilcox, are rated for 750 psi design and 750F superheated steam. Nationwide Boiler will also provide a plant master workstation and one deaerator tank with pumps for a minimum one-year rental during the refinery's commissioning phase.

The California-based distributor secured the contract with equipment already in stock and available for immediate shipment, a key competitive advantage. William Testa, Nationwide Boiler's Director of Sales, said the company's success hinged on immediate availability combined with preparation and collaboration. "This multifaceted rental will require continued collaboration between our project manager, export logistics and transportation company, and the customer," Testa said.

Nationwide Boiler was assisted by its new Middle East representative, Aujan Industrial Solutions, and EPC customer Redland Industrial Services (Arabia) Ltd.

The equipment will ship from Houston via roll-on/roll-off vessel with an expected six-week transit time to the Port of Jizan. The refinery spans 106 square kilometers and is designed to process more than 400,000 barrels per day of crude oil for domestic and international customers. The facility will operate as an entirely self-sufficient complex, providing power to locally owned manufacturing and service companies.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/nationwide-boiler-inc-secures-largest-rental-project-in-their-fifty-one-year-history/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/nationwide-boiler-inc-secures-largest-rental-project-in-their-fifty-one-year-history/ "NATIONWIDE BOILER INC. SECURES LARGEST RENTAL PROJECT IN THEIR FIFTY-ONE YEAR HISTORY"

title: ACWA Power Wins First Utility-Scale Renewable Energy Project in Saudi Arabia
content:
ACWA Power has been awarded Saudi Arabia's first utility-scale renewable energy project, a 300 MW solar facility in Al Jouf that achieved a world record tariff of 8.782 halala/kWh, equivalent to U.S. 2.34 cents/kWh.

The Skaka solar plant marks the opening phase of Saudi Arabia's national renewable energy program, which aims to generate 9.5 GW of renewable capacity by 2023. The project secured a 25-year power purchase contract with an investment value of SR 993,750 million, approximately U.S. $256 million, across a site spanning more than six square kilometers.

Mohammad Abdullah Abunayyan, chairman of ACWA Power, said the company was "humbled to be entrusted with the first utility-scale renewable energy plant to be developed in the Kingdom." He added that the project aligns with Saudi Vision 2030 and the National Transition Plan 2020 objectives.

ACWA Power, which operates power generation and desalination plants across 11 countries, has previously developed renewable projects in the United Arab Emirates, Jordan, Egypt, Morocco and South Africa. Abunayyan stated the company would "bring to the Kingdom our considerable expertise and capabilities to ensure the delivery of this first renewable energy project at the highest global standards of quality and efficiency."

The renewable energy procurement was managed by the Renewable Energy Projects Development Office, or REPDO.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-wins-first-utility-scale-renewable-energy-project-in-saudi-arabia/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-wins-first-utility-scale-renewable-energy-project-in-saudi-arabia/ "ACWA Power Wins First Utility-Scale Renewable Energy Project in Saudi Arabia"

title: Wärtsilä ensures the reliability and availability of Saudi Arabian power plant
content:
Wärtsilä signed a three-year asset management agreement with Northern Region Cement Company to operate and maintain NRCC's 62 MW power plant in Turaif, Saudi Arabia. The deal, signed in October 2017, extends Wärtsilä's service relationship with the cement company dating back to 2008.

Under the agreement, Wärtsilä will oversee daily operations, preventive and predictive maintenance of the facility. The company's Customer Centre in Dubai will remotely monitor the plant's condition to provide technical advisory and data analysis services. This monitoring enables knowledge-based decisions to optimize reliability and efficiency.

"Wärtsilä has been responsible for the operation and maintenance of our power plant already since 2008. We have been very satisfied with the quality of Wärtsilä's service and support as well as the way they ensure safe and reliable operations," said Dr. Obaid Alsobiei, CEO of Northern Region Cement Company.

Wärtsilä has previously conducted electrical and automation upgrades to enhance the plant's performance and extend its operational life. The company said the arrangement has ensured reliable operations, optimized fuel consumption and reduced maintenance costs for NRCC.

"By having full responsibility for the operation and maintenance of NRCC's power plant, we have been able to ensure reliable operations, optimised fuel consumption and reduced maintenance costs for NRCC," said Haidar al Hertani, Managing Director of Wärtsilä Saudi Arabia.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-ensures-the-reliability-and-availability-of-saudi-arabian-power-plant/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-ensures-the-reliability-and-availability-of-saudi-arabian-power-plant/ "Wärtsilä ensures the reliability and availability of Saudi Arabian power plant"

title: Wärtsilä to supply 38 MW power plant to Saudi Arabia
content:
Wärtsilä will supply a 38 MW power plant to a Saudi Arabian customer under a turn-key engineering, procurement and construction order booked in the first quarter of 2017. The plant will include four Wärtsilä 32TS engines, a two-stage turbocharged version optimized for extreme ambient conditions.

The 32TS engine is designed to deliver high efficiency with reduced fuel and water consumption in high altitudes and hot temperatures while meeting World Bank Environment, Health and Safety guidelines for nitrogen oxide emissions. The power plant will be the first Wärtsilä 32TS installation to operate primarily on crude oil, with heavy and light fuel oils as backup fuels.

The project aligns with Saudi Vision 2030, the country's transformation programme aimed at improving energy efficiency and increasing the role of gas and renewables in the energy mix.

"In addition to the very high efficiency, the main cornerstones of our solutions are exceptional operational flexibility, multi-fuel capability, and minimal water consumption, even in hot conditions," said Qasim Latif, Business Development Manager at Wärtsilä Energy Solutions. "Together these features, along with the ability to act as a state-of-the-art enabler of renewable energy integration, make our solutions very well-suited for the Saudi Arabian market."

Wärtsilä has maintained operations in Saudi Arabia for more than 40 years. Upon completion of this project, the company will have more than 1,900 MW of installed capacity in the country. Globally, Wärtsilä's installed base exceeds 63 GW across 176 countries.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-to-supply-38-mw-power-plant-to-saudi-arabia/

title: MAN Diesel & Turbo builds power plant in Saudi Arabia
content:
MAN Diesel & Turbo has secured an engineering, procurement and construction contract to build a 40 MW power plant for Arabian Cement Company in Rabigh, Saudi Arabia, about 120 km north of Jeddah on the Red Sea coast.

The plant will use two MAN 18V48/60 TS engines and operate on heavy fuel oil and diesel oil to supply power to a cement factory. The engines feature two-stage turbocharging, which the company said improves efficiency, power density and turbocharging performance compared to single-turbocharger models.

Construction of the first phase is scheduled for 2016 and 2017. Arabian Cement Company plans to expand capacity to 120 MW in two subsequent construction phases by installing four additional engines.

Gasem Al Maimani, president and CEO of Arabian Cement Company, said MAN Diesel & Turbo had been "a reliable partner of ours for many years" and praised the company's professionalism, engine quality and flexibility in meeting customer requirements.

As EPC consortium leader, MAN Diesel & Turbo will oversee the entire plant construction. Ashraf Al Natsheh, senior sales director for power plants in Saudi Arabia, said the order represented "another milestone in the development of our EPC business in Saudi Arabia" and reflected strong relationships with the local cement and building industry.

In 2016, MAN will also deliver a 55 MW power plant to United Cement Industrial Company, which will be operated by PrimeServ, MAN's service brand.

Alexander Stöckler, global head of sales at the MAN Diesel & Turbo power plant division, said Saudi Arabia was becoming a core market for the company.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/man-diesel-turbo-builds-power-plant-in-saudi-arabia/
